findings.description: u_0004 (parent company sign-off lives in locked footer; do not add in sections) has no corresponding candidate rule at all — an entire compliance/governance statement about footer placement and prohibition on duplicating sign-off in body sections is missing from the cluster.

payload.text: Do not add the parent company (Priovant Therapeutics, Inc.) sign-off within document sections; it must only appear in the locked footer.

findings.description: Rule captures the ALL CAPS requirement but omits governance facets: (1) explicit scope restriction to 'patient marketing' materials, and (2) the supersedes relation to the Feb 2026 general guide's title-case rule, which is a key precedence/override fact.

This single rule conflates four distinct governance facets from one source unit: (a) conditional inclusion of ® only when generic name present, (b) placement restriction to first/most prominent mention only, (c) scope restriction to external-facing documents only, and (d) an explicit must_not constraint against repeating ® on subsequent mentions. The negative constraint (do not repeat) has opposite polarity to the rest and is being silently absorbed into a single 'must' rule, obscuring a distinct compliance obligation.

new_rules.scope: external-facing documents only, first/most prominent mention only
new_rules.text: Include ® only when generic name is included, on first/most prominent mention, in external-facing documents only.
Do not repeat ® on subsequent mentions after the first/most prominent mention.

findings.description: Missed relation: the generic name format rule (lowercase, parenthetical) is directly referenced and required by the trademark symbol pairing rule (® applies only when generic name is included, e.g., LISRAYA® (brepocitinib)). These two rules are dependent/co-occurring but no relation is recorded between them.
No scope facet recorded (e.g., whether this format applies to all materials or only external-facing ones like the adjacent trademark rule); ambiguous scope could cause inconsistent enforcement.
